text: Restroom shutoff valve above the upper terminal water closet, behind a locked access panel — keyed panel at student restrooms, unkeyed at faculty restrooms.

citations.note: RR shutoff above upper terminal WC behind locked panel (keyed for students, unkeyed for faculty).

text: Battery-powered infrared flush valves with manual override; hardwired flush sensors are not permitted.

citations.note: IR battery flush valves w/ manual override (NO hardwire).

text: Push-button self-metering lavatory faucets; ADA operation ≥10 seconds at ≤5 lb force.

citations.note: Push-button metered faucets.

text: Slope restroom floors to floor drains at 1/8 in. per foot.

citations.note: Floor drains w/ primers + sloped floors; floor-drain floors slope 1/8 in./ft.

text: Full-size cleanout above every urinal, lavatory, drinking fountain, bottle filler and sink; at lavatories ≤6 in. above the backsplash, aligned to the fixture centerline (offset where a mirror occurs).

citations.note: Cleanouts above every urinal, lav, DF, bottle filler, sink; lav CO at centerline ≤6 in. above backsplash, offset for mirrors.

text: Cleanout above the upper-terminal water closet where multiple water closets occur on a branch.

citations.note: Upper-terminal WC cleanout only when multiple WCs.

text: Water hammer arrestor (WHA-1) on the fixture header; air chambers are not approved.

citations.note: Mechanical water hammer arrestors only; air chambers not approved.

text: Isolation valve within the room, 3–7 ft AFF, in a recessed locked box, controlling only this room.

citations.note: Isolation valves: every room with fixtures (3–7 ft AFF, control only that room, recessed locked box).

text: Isolation valves per floor in multi-story buildings.

citations.note: Per-floor isolation valves.

text: Back-to-back restrooms are isolated separately.

citations.note: Back-to-back restrooms isolated separately.

text: Maximum two valves per access panel.

citations.note: Max 2 valves per access panel.

text: Provide future filter provision at every drinking fountain / bottle filler: DFWF-1 filter head + bypass in a locked panel.
applies_to: All drinking fountains and bottle fillers

citations.note: All DF/BF: future filter provision (DFWF-1 head + bypass in locked panel).

text: Brass-free waterways at all drinking fountains and bottle fillers.
applies_to: All drinking fountains and bottle fillers

citations.note: All DF/BF: brass-free waterways.

text: Recessed hose bibb beneath each exterior drinking fountain.

citations.note: Hose bibbs under exterior DFs.
Exterior units vandal-resistant w/ recessed hose bibb beneath.

text: Exterior fountain/bottle-filler units are vandal-resistant; sun-exposed stainless receives solar-reflective powder coat.
applies_to: Exterior drinking fountains and bottle fillers

citations.note: Sun-exposed stainless gets solar-reflective powder coat.

text: Locate fountains/bottle fillers in CBC-compliant alcoves on accessible routes.
applies_to: Fountains/bottle fillers at corridors and walkways

citations.note: Alcove rules (CBC) on accessible routes.

text: Lunch-shelter pavilion drains: cast iron with basket strainer, no trap primer — daily washdown keeps traps charged.

citations.note: Lunch shelters: CI w/ basket, no primer.

text: Tempered water regulating valve (TMV) near the outlets served; locate in the custodial room or similar — not inside the restroom.
applies_to: Tempered water service (student restroom lavatories and similar)

citations.note: Tempered 95–100°F; TMV near outlet but NOT in restroom — custodial room.

text: Hot water 120°F at the heater / 115°F at the furthest outlet; tempered water 95–100°F; tepid water 60–100°F.

citations.note: 120°F at heater / 115°F furthest outlet; tempered 95–100°F; tepid 60–100°F.

text: Lavatory header sizing: ½ in. for 2 lavatories, ¾ in. for 3–4, 1 in. for 5–6.

citations.note: Lavs ½/¾/1 in. for 2 / 3–4 / 5–6.

text: Insulate exposed piping under accessible lavatories (Plumberex / Lav-Guard type).

citations.note: Insulation under accessible lavs (Plumberex/Lav-Guard).

text: Steel backing plate at wall-hung lavatories.

citations.note: Steel backing plate spec.

text: Through-wall supplies are IPS brass nipple only (no braided/flex connectors, no copper MIP sweat adapters); loose-key square-shank lock-shield stops; each faucet on its own ¼-turn angle stop.

citations.note: IPS brass nipple only; no braided SS flex supplies; loose-key square-shank lock-shield stops; ¼-turn angle stop per faucet.

text: Trap primer at each interior floor drain.
applies_to: Interior floor drains (lunch-shelter pavilion drains excepted)

citations.note: Floor drains w/ trap primers.

text: Sill cock in boiler/mechanical rooms.

citations.note: Sill cocks in boiler/mech rooms + roofs w/ PV/skylights/HVAC.

text: Emergency shower/eyewash served with tepid water (60–100°F) from a potable connection.

citations.note: Tepid water; potable connection.
Tepid only: emergency shower/eyewash.
